title: Colour Palettes
text: UtilsR provides palette system 256+ colour schemes RColorBrewer, ggsci, viridis, rcartocolor, nord, . Core components:

title: Colour Palettes
text: built-default palette used plt_cat() plot functions:
code: as.character(pal_lancet) #>  [1] "#00468BFF" "#ED0000FF" "#42B540FF" "#0099B4FF" "#925E9FFF" "#FDAF91FF" #>  [7] "#AD002AFF" "#ADB6B6FF" "#1B1919FF" "#79AF97FF" "#DF8F44FF" "#6A6599FF" #> [13] "#FCCDE5FF" "#80B1D3FF" "#0000FFFF" as.character(pal_lancet[1:5]) #> [1] "#00468BFF" "#ED0000FF" "#42B540FF" "#0099B4FF" "#925E9FFF" ggplot(iris, aes(Sepal.Length, Sepal.Width, color = Species)) +   geom_point(size = 2) +   scale_color_manual(values = as.character(pal_lancet[1:3])) +   theme_my()

title: Colour Palettes
text: palettes stored palette_list (internal data, accessed via pal_get()):
code: # Browse available palette names head(names(UtilsR:::palette_list), 20) #>  [1] "BrBG"     "PiYG"     "PRGn"     "PuOr"     "RdBu"     "RdGy"     #>  [7] "RdYlBu"   "RdYlGn"   "Spectral" "Accent"   "Dark2"    "Paired"   #> [13] "Pastel1"  "Pastel2"  "Set1"     "Set2"     "Set3"     "Blues"    #> [19] "BuGn"     "BuPu" length(UtilsR:::palette_list) #> [1] 256

title: Colour Palettes
text: Retrieve colours palette name: Use ggplot2:
code: # Get 5 colours from "Paired" pal_get("Paired", n = 5) #>  #A6CEE3   #1F78B4   #B2DF8A   #33A02C   #FB9A99   # Get 3 colours from "Set1" pal_get("Set1", n = 3) #>  #E41A1C   #377EB8   #4DAF4A   # Get 8 colours from "Dark2" pal_get("Dark2", n = 8) #>  #1B9E77   #D95F02   #7570B3   #E7298A   #66A61E   #E6AB02   #A6761D   #666666 ggplot(iris, aes(Sepal.Length, Sepal.Width, color = Species)) +   geom_point(size = 2) +   scale_color_manual(values = as.character(pal_get("Set2", n = 3))) +   theme_my()

title: Colour Palettes
text: Display colour swatches palette:
code: pal_show("Paired") pal_show("Set1")

title: Colour Palettes
code: # Interactive palette browser (opens viewer) pal_list()

title: Colour Palettes
code: show_color(c("#FF6B6B", "#4ECDC4", "#45B7D1", "#96CEB4", "#FFEAA7"))

title: Colour Palettes
text: Create palette object auto-display:
code: my_pal <- as_palette(c("#264653", "#2A9D8F", "#E9C46A", "#F4A261", "#E76F51")) as.character(my_pal)  # display as plain hex vector #> [1] "#264653" "#2A9D8F" "#E9C46A" "#F4A261" "#E76F51"

title: Colour Palettes
text: Palettes integrate seamlessly plot functions:
code: set.seed(1) df <- data.frame(Type = factor(sample(LETTERS[1:5], 200, TRUE)))  # Use named palette plt_cat(df, "Type", type = "pie", label = TRUE, palette = "Set2") # Use custom colours plt_cat(df, "Type", type = "bar", stat = "count",         palette = c("#264653", "#2A9D8F", "#E9C46A", "#F4A261", "#E76F51")) # Default (pal_lancet) — no palette argument needed plt_cat(df, "Type", type = "pie", label = TRUE)

title: Factor Manipulation: fct_cat, fct_num
text: factor operations one function. Works inside dplyr::mutate().

title: Factor Manipulation: fct_cat, fct_num
code: x <- factor(c("I", "II", "III", "IV"))  # Recode by name fct_cat(x, early = c("I", "II"), late = c("III", "IV")) #> [1] early early late  late  #> Levels: early late  # Recode by position fct_cat(x, g1 = 1:2, g2 = 3:4) #> [1] g1 g1 g2 g2 #> Levels: g1 g2

title: Factor Manipulation: fct_cat, fct_num
code: # Move specific levels to front fct_cat(x, "III", "I") #> [1]    II    IV #> Levels:  II IV

title: plt_cat: Unified Categorical Plot Guide
code: library(UtilsR) library(ggplot2)  set.seed(1) df <- data.frame(   Type  = factor(sample(c("A","B","C"), 200, TRUE, c(0.5, 0.3, 0.2))),   Group = factor(sample(c("X","Y","Z"), 200, TRUE)),   Batch = factor(sample(c("B1","B2"), 200, TRUE)) ) df$Region <- factor(ifelse(df$Group %in% c("X","Y"), "R1", "R2")) head(df) #>   Type Group Batch Region #> 1    A     Y    B2     R1 #> 2    A     X    B2     R1 #> 3    B     Y    B1     R1 #> 4    C     Y    B1     R1 #> 5    A     Y    B1     R1 #> 6    C     Z    B1     R2

title: plt_cat: Unified Categorical Plot Guide
text: common type. Supports stat = "percent" (default) "count", position = "stack" (default) "dodge".
code: plt_cat(df, "Type", "Group", type = "bar") plt_cat(df, "Type", "Group", type = "bar",         stat = "count", position = "dodge", label = TRUE) plt_cat(df, "Type", "Group", type = "bar",         flip = TRUE, label = TRUE)

title: plt_cat: Unified Categorical Plot Guide
text: group.omitted, stat.level becomes bar:
code: plt_cat(df, "Type", type = "bar", stat = "count",         label = TRUE, legend.position = "none")

title: plt_cat: Unified Categorical Plot Guide
text: Use bg.dodge mode add alternating background colour bands. bg.must superset group.(group belongs exactly one bg level).
code: plt_cat(df, "Type", "Group", type = "bar",         position = "dodge", bg.by = "Region") plt_cat(df, "Type", "Group", type = "bar",         position = "dodge", bg.by = "Region",         bg_palette = c("lightblue", "lightyellow"),         title = "Custom Background Colors")

title: plt_cat: Unified Categorical Plot Guide
text: bar chart polar coordinates (theta = "x"). Good cyclical data.
code: plt_cat(df, "Type", "Group", type = "rose") plt_cat(df, "Type", "Group", type = "rose",         stat = "count", position = "dodge")

title: plt_cat: Unified Categorical Plot Guide
text: pie chart hole center. Supports dodge grouped spiral display.
code: plt_cat(df, "Type", type = "ring", label = TRUE) plt_cat(df, "Type", "Group", type = "ring",         stat = "count", position = "dodge")

title: plt_cat: Unified Categorical Plot Guide
code: plt_cat(df, "Type", type = "pie", label = TRUE) plt_cat(df, "Type", type = "pie",         palette = "Paired",         title = "Type Distribution",         subtitle = "n = 200",         legend.position = "bottom",         legend.direction = "horizontal")

title: plt_cat: Unified Categorical Plot Guide
text: Combines stepped area background bar overlay. Best showing trends across ordered groups.
code: plt_cat(df, "Type", "Group", type = "trend", stat = "count") plt_cat(df, "Type", "Group", type = "trend",         stat = "count", label = TRUE)

title: plt_cat: Unified Categorical Plot Guide
text: Stacked area chart. Uses continuous x-axis internally.
code: plt_cat(df, "Type", "Group", type = "area", stat = "count") plt_cat(df, "Type", "Group", type = "area",         stat = "count", position = "dodge")

title: plt_cat: Unified Categorical Plot Guide
text: Bubble-style dot plot size encodes value.
code: plt_cat(df, "Type", "Group", type = "dot") plt_cat(df, "Type", "Group", type = "dot",         label = TRUE, stat = "count")

title: plt_cat: Unified Categorical Plot Guide
text: Requires stat.>= 2 column names. Shows flow categories.
code: plt_cat(df, c("Type", "Group"), type = "sankey") plt_cat(df, c("Type", "Group", "Batch"), type = "sankey")

title: plt_cat: Unified Categorical Plot Guide
text: Shows pairwise connections two categorical variables. Returns base R graphics (ggplot).
code: plt_cat(df, c("Type", "Group"), type = "chord")

title: plt_cat: Unified Categorical Plot Guide
text: Shows set intersections. Use stat_level specify level counts “positive”.
code: set.seed(42) df_bin <- data.frame(   A = sample(c("Yes","No"), 200, TRUE),   B = sample(c("Yes","No"), 200, TRUE),   C = sample(c("Yes","No"), 200, TRUE) ) plt_cat(df_bin, c("A","B","C"), type = "venn") plt_cat(df, c("Type", "Group"), type = "venn",         stat_level = c("A", "X"))

title: plt_cat: Unified Categorical Plot Guide
text: scalable alternative Venn diagrams showing set intersections.
code: plt_cat(df_bin, c("A","B","C"), type = "upset") plt_cat(df, c("Type", "Group"), type = "upset",         stat_level = c("A", "X"))

title: plt_cat: Unified Categorical Plot Guide
text: Split data variable create one panel per level:
code: plt_cat(df, "Type", "Group", split.by = "Batch", type = "bar") plt_cat(df, "Type", split.by = "Batch", type = "pie",         facet_ncol = 2)

title: plt_cat: Unified Categorical Plot Guide
text: Include NA values plot NA_stat = TRUE:
code: df_na <- df df_na$Type[1:20] <- NA plt_cat(df_na, "Type", "Group", type = "bar",         NA_stat = TRUE, NA_color = "red")

title: plt_cat: Unified Categorical Plot Guide
text: Preserve dropped factor levels empty bars:
code: df2 <- df[df$Type != "C", , drop = FALSE] plt_cat(df2, "Type", "Group", type = "bar", keep_empty = TRUE)

title: plt_cat: Unified Categorical Plot Guide
text: Customise label appearance label.fg, label.bg, label.bg.r:
code: plt_cat(df, "Type", "Group", type = "bar",         stat = "count", position = "dodge",         label = TRUE, label.fg = "white", label.bg = "black")

title: plt_cat: Unified Categorical Plot Guide
text: Control panel aspect ratio (auto-set 1 polar types):
code: plt_cat(df, "Type", "Group", type = "bar", aspect.ratio = 0.5)

title: plt_cat: Unified Categorical Plot Guide
code: plt_cat(df, "Type", type = "pie",         palette = c("#FF6B6B", "#4ECDC4", "#45B7D1"),         label = TRUE)

code: pak::pak("HUI950319/UtilsR")  # or devtools::install_github("HUI950319/UtilsR") # Sankey pak::pak("davidsjoberg/ggsankey")  # Chord, Venn, UpSet install.packages(c("circlize", "ggVennDiagram", "ggupset"))

title: Utility Functions for Data Exploration and System Diagnostics
text: plt_cat() – One function, 11 chart types (bar, pie, ring, rose, dot, trend, area, sankey, chord, venn, upset), 27+ parameters plt_con() – One function, 4 chart types (violin, box, bar, dot) statistical comparisons, overlays, split/stack layout lv() – Instant variable summary data.frame Seurat objects grouping cross-tabulation fmt_*() – Chainable ggplot2 formatting: axes, tags, legends, reference lines, strips, comparisons, backgrounds, scales, 256 built-palettes via pal_get() / pal_list(), plus 11 hand-picked palettes (pal_lancet, pal_ditto, pal_igv, …) fct_cat() – Recode, reorder, reverse, binarise, group, combine factors one call

title: Utility Functions for Data Exploration and System Diagnostics
code: library(UtilsR)  set.seed(1) df <- data.frame(   Type  = factor(sample(c("A","B","C"), 200, TRUE)),   Group = factor(sample(c("X","Y","Z"), 200, TRUE)),   Batch = factor(sample(c("B1","B2"), 200, TRUE)) )  plt_cat(df, "Type", "Group", type = "bar")                           # stacked percent plt_cat(df, "Type", "Group", type = "bar",         stat = "count", position = "dodge", label = TRUE)            # dodged count plt_cat(df, "Type", type = "pie", label = TRUE)                     # pie chart plt_cat(df, "Type", "Group", type = "ring",         stat = "count", position = "dodge")                          # donut chart plt_cat(df, "Type", "Group", type = "rose")                         # rose (wind rose) plt_cat(df, "Type", "Group", type = "dot")                          # Cleveland dot plt_cat(df, "Type", "Group", type = "trend", stat = "count")        # trend line plt_cat(df, "Type", "Group", type = "area", stat = "count")         # area chart  # Multi-variable: sankey, chord, venn, upset plt_cat(df, c("Type", "Group"), type = "sankey") plt_cat(df, c("Type", "Group"), type = "chord") plt_cat(df, c("Type", "Group"), type = "venn", stat_level = c("A", "X")) plt_cat(df, c("Type", "Group"), type = "upset", stat_level = c("A", "X"))  # Split by facet + background bands plt_cat(df, "Type", "Group", split.by = "Batch", type = "bar")

title: Utility Functions for Data Exploration and System Diagnostics
code: set.seed(1) df2 <- data.frame(   value = rnorm(200),   score = rnorm(200, mean = 2),   group = factor(sample(c("A", "B", "C"), 200, TRUE)),   batch = factor(sample(c("B1", "B2"), 200, TRUE)) )  # Basic types plt_con(df2, "value", "group")                          # violin (default) plt_con(df2, "value", "group", type = "box")            # boxplot plt_con(df2, "value", "group", type = "bar")            # mean +/- SD plt_con(df2, "value", "group", type = "dot")            # bubble dot plot  # Overlays plt_con(df2, "value", "group", add_box = TRUE, add_point = TRUE)  # Statistical comparisons plt_con(df2, "value", "group",         comparisons = list(c("A", "B"), c("A", "C")))  # Multiple features + stack plt_con(df2, c("value", "score"), "group", stack = TRUE)  # Split by batch plt_con(df2, "value", "group", split.by = "batch")

title: Unified Factor Manipulation — fct_cat
text: One function factor operations. Works vectors (usable mutate()) auto-detects action arguments.

title: Unified Factor Manipulation — fct_cat
code: fct_cat(   x,   ...,   reverse = FALSE,   binary_ref = NULL,   groups = NULL,   new_labels = NULL,   combine = NULL,   sep = " & " )

title: Unified Factor Manipulation — fct_cat
text: x factor character vector. combine mode optional – missing, columns listed combine used. ... recode: named args (new = old). reorder: level names desired order (unnamed strings). Must named unnamed. reverse Logical. Reverse levels? Default FALSE. binary_ref Integer index(es) reference level(s). Others collapse "Oth". groups Named list integer index vectors custom grouping, e.g. list(early = 1:2, late = 3:4). new_labels Character vector rename levels (length levels). combine Character vector column names combine. Works inside mutate(). x provided, combined listed columns. x missing, listed columns combined. sep Separator combine. Default " & ".

title: Unified Factor Manipulation — fct_cat
text: factor.

title: Unified Factor Manipulation — fct_cat
code: x <- factor(letters[1:6])  # Recode (named ...) fct_cat(x, AB = c("a","b"), CD = c("c","d")) #> [1] AB AB CD CD e  f  #> Levels: AB CD e f fct_cat(x, g1 = 1:3, g2 = 4:6) #> [1] g1 g1 g1 g2 g2 g2 #> Levels: g1 g2  # Reorder (unnamed ...) fct_cat(factor(letters[1:4]), "c", "b") #> [1] a     d #> Levels:  a d  # Reverse fct_cat(factor(letters[1:4]), reverse = TRUE) #> [1] a b c d #> Levels: d c b a  # Binary fct_cat(factor(c("I","II","III","IV")), binary_ref = 1) #> [1] I   Oth Oth Oth #> Levels: I Oth  # Group fct_cat(factor(c("I","II","III","IV")), groups = list(early = 1:2, late = 3:4)) #> [1] early early late  late  #> Levels: early late  # Relabel fct_cat(factor(c("I","II","III","IV")), new_labels = c("One","Two","Three","Four")) #> [1] One   Two   Three Four  #> Levels: One Two Three Four  # Combine in mutate(): if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ df %>% mutate(grp = fct_cat(sex, combine = "age")) df %>% mutate(grp = fct_cat(sex, combine = c("age", "race"))) df %>% mutate(grp = fct_cat(combine = c("sex", "age"))) } # }

title: Convert Numeric to Factor — fct_num
text: Cut numeric vector factor groups. Works vectors (usable mutate()). Supports fixed cut points automatic binning.

title: Convert Numeric to Factor — fct_num
code: fct_num(   x,   breaks = NULL,   labels = NULL,   nbins = NULL,   type = c("quantile", "equal") )

title: Convert Numeric to Factor — fct_num
text: x numeric vector. breaks Numeric vector cut points. E.g. 50 binary, c(20, 50) 3 groups. Mutually exclusive nbins. labels Character vector group labels. NULL, labels auto-generated (e.g. "below_50", "50-80", "above_80"). nbins Integer number bins automatic binning. Mutually exclusive breaks. type Binning method using nbins: "quantile" (default) "equal" width.

title: Convert Numeric to Factor — fct_num
text: factor.

title: Convert Numeric to Factor — fct_num
code: x <- c(10, 25, 40, 55, 70, 85, 100)  # Fixed cut points fct_num(x, breaks = 50) #> [1] below_50 below_50 below_50 above_50 above_50 above_50 above_50 #> Levels: below_50 above_50 fct_num(x, breaks = c(30, 70)) #> [1] below_30 below_30 30-70    30-70    30-70    above_70 above_70 #> Levels: below_30 30-70 above_70 fct_num(x, breaks = c(30, 70), labels = c("Low", "Mid", "High")) #> [1] Low  Low  Mid  Mid  Mid  High High #> Levels: Low Mid High  # Automatic binning fct_num(x, nbins = 3) #> [1] low    low    low    middle middle high   high   #> Levels: low middle high fct_num(x, nbins = 2, labels = c("Low", "High")) #> [1] Low  Low  Low  Low  High High High #> Levels: Low High fct_num(x, nbins = 4, type = "equal") #> [1] low       low       medium    medium    high      very_high very_high #> Levels: low medium high very_high  # In mutate(): if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ df %>% mutate(age_grp = fct_num(age, breaks = c(40, 60))) df %>% mutate(age_q4 = fct_num(age, nbins = 4)) } # }

title: Flatten nested patchwork objects — flatten_patchwork

title: Flatten nested patchwork objects — flatten_patchwork
text: plots patchwork object (possibly nested) list ggplot objects. ... Arguments passed [patchwork::wrap_plots()], e.g. `nrow`, `ncol`. select_inds Integer vector indices keep flattening. nrow_inds_order Single integer reorder plots interleaving rows.

title: Flatten nested patchwork objects — flatten_patchwork
text: patchwork object sub-plots one level.

title: Flatten nested patchwork objects — flatten_patchwork
code: library(ggplot2) library(patchwork) p1 <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(mpg, disp)) + geom_point() p2 <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(gear, disp, group = gear)) + geom_boxplot() p3 <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(factor(gear))) + geom_bar() p4 <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(factor(cyl), mpg)) + geom_violin()  # \donttest{ # Flatten nested patchwork nested <- (p1 | p2) / (p3 | p4) flatten_patchwork(nested, nrow = 1)   # Select specific sub-plots flatten_patchwork(nested, select_inds = c(1, 3))  # }  # Reorder by rows (column-first -> row-first) nested2 <- (p1 | p2) / (p3 | p4) flatten_patchwork(nested2, nrow_inds_order = 2, ncol = 2)

what: Add Data Points to a Plot — fmt_point
title: Add Data Points to a Plot — fmt_point
text: Unified function overlay data points ggplot objects. Supports plain points, jittered points, beeswarm layout. Automatically detects group aesthetics apply dodge positioning.

title: Add Data Points to a Plot — fmt_point
code: fmt_point(   plot,   type = c("point", "jitter", "beeswarm"),   data = NULL,   shape = 19,   size = 1,   white_border = FALSE,   dodge.width = 0.8,   jitter.args = list(width = 0.2, height = 0),   beeswarm.args = list(cex = 3, corral = "wrap", corral.width = 0.5),   sample_n = NULL,   seed = 42,   rasterize = FALSE,   rasterize.dpi = 300,   ... )

title: Add Data Points to a Plot — fmt_point
text: plot ggplot, patchwork, list ggplot objects. type Point layout type: "point" (default), "jitter", "beeswarm". data Data point layer. Default NULL inherits plot. Can data.frame function/formula applied plot data (e.g. ~ subset(.x, value > 10)). shape Point shape. Default 19 (solid circle). size Point size. Default 1. white_border Logical. TRUE, converts shapes fillable versions (21-24) white stroke. Default FALSE. dodge.width Dodge width grouped data. Default 0.8. Set 0 disable dodging. jitter.args Named list jitter arguments. used type = "jitter". Defaults: list(width = 0.2, height = 0). beeswarm.args Named list beeswarm arguments. used type = "beeswarm". Defaults: list(cex = 3, corral = "wrap", corral.width = 0.5). sample_n Integer NULL. set, randomly subsample data plotting reduce overplotting. plot group aesthetic (color/fill), sampling stratified: sample_n points taken per group. Groups fewer points kept full. Default NULL (sampling). seed Random seed reproducible sampling. Default 42. rasterize Logical. TRUE, rasterizes point layer via ggrastr::rasterise large datasets. Default FALSE. rasterize.dpi Integer, DPI rasterization. Default 300. ... Additional arguments passed geom_point geom_beeswarm.

title: Add Data Points to a Plot — fmt_point
text: type input (ggplot, patchwork, list).

title: Add Data Points to a Plot — fmt_point
code: library(ggplot2) p <- ggplot(iris, aes(Species, Sepal.Length)) + geom_boxplot()  # Plain points fmt_point(p, color = "steelblue", alpha = 0.5)   # Jittered points fmt_point(p, type = "jitter", color = "red", alpha = 0.5)   # Custom jitter width fmt_point(p, type = "jitter", jitter.args = list(width = 0.4), alpha = 0.5)   # Beeswarm fmt_point(p, type = "beeswarm", color = "darkgreen", size = 1.2)   # White border effect fmt_point(p, type = "jitter", fill = "purple",           white_border = TRUE, size = 2)   # Grouped data (auto-dodge) p2 <- ggplot(iris, aes(Species, Sepal.Length, color = Species)) +   geom_boxplot() fmt_point(p2, type = "jitter", alpha = 0.5)   # Subsample when too many points fmt_point(p, type = "jitter", sample_n = 20, alpha = 0.5)   # Stratified sampling (per group) fmt_point(p2, type = "jitter", sample_n = 10, alpha = 0.5)

title: Compound assignment pipe operator — %<>%
text: Pipes lhs rhs, assigns result back lhs. Equivalent lhs <- lhs %>% rhs.

title: Compound assignment pipe operator — %<>%
code: lhs %<>% rhs

title: Compound assignment pipe operator — %<>%
text: lhs variable name (updated calling environment). rhs function expression pipe .

title: Compound assignment pipe operator — %<>%
text: Invisibly returns new value lhs.

title: Not-in operator — %ni%
text: Negation %%. Returns TRUE elements x table.

title: Not-in operator — %ni%
code: x %ni% table

title: Not-in operator — %ni%
text: x Vector values check. table Vector values match .

title: Not-in operator — %ni%
text: Logical vector.

title: Variable Inspection Generic Function — lv
text: Inspect variables data.frame Seurat object, including frequency counts, unique values, missing values special values (e.g. "unknown", "blank", "n/"). Supports grouped summaries multi-variable cross-tabulation.

title: Variable Inspection Generic Function — lv
code: lv(data, ...)  # Default S3 method lv(data, ..., pattern = NULL, group = NULL, count = NULL)  # S3 method for class 'data.frame' lv(data, ..., pattern = NULL, group = NULL, count = NULL)  # S3 method for class 'Seurat' lv(   data,   ...,   pattern = NULL,   group = NULL,   count = NULL,   add_reductions = FALSE,   add_cell_stats = TRUE )

title: Variable Inspection Generic Function — lv
text: data data object: data.frame Seurat object. ... Variable selection using dplyr::select syntax. pattern Optional regex pattern matching variable names. group Optional grouping variable. Supports NSE (bare name) character string. Seurat objects, set TRUE use current Ident. count Optional variable(s) cross-tabulation. Accepts single variable name (NSE string), multiple variable names via c(var1, var2) (NSE) character vector c("var1", "var2"). Independent group. add_reductions Logical. Whether add reduction coordinates. Default FALSE. add_cell_stats Logical. Whether show cell statistics. Default TRUE.

title: Variable Inspection Generic Function — lv
text: Invisibly returns input data.

title: Variable Inspection Generic Function — lv
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # data.frame basic usage lv(mtcars) lv(mtcars, mpg, cyl, hp) lv(mtcars, pattern = "mpg")  # Grouped view (supports NSE) lv(mtcars, mpg, hp, wt, group = cyl) lv(mtcars, mpg, hp, wt, group = "cyl")  # Cross-tabulation lv(mtcars, count = cyl) lv(mtcars, count = c(cyl, gear)) lv(mtcars, count = c("cyl", "gear"))  # Seurat object lv(seurat_obj) lv(seurat_obj, group = TRUE) lv(seurat_obj, count = c(seurat_clusters, Phase)) } # }

title: Pipe operator — %>%
text: See dplyr::%>% details. Return lhs NULL, otherwise rhs.

what: Get Colours from a Named Palette — pal_get
title: Get Colours from a Named Palette — pal_get
text: Retrieve colours built-palette collection (256 palettes). Supports discrete mapping (factor/character input) continuous interpolation (numeric input n parameter).

title: Get Colours from a Named Palette — pal_get
code: pal_get(palette = "Paired", n = NULL, x = NULL, reverse = FALSE, alpha = 1)

title: Get Colours from a Named Palette — pal_get
text: palette Palette name (e.g. "Paired", "viridis", "lancet"). Use pal_list() see available names. n Number colours return. discrete palettes, colours recycled interpolated needed. Default NULL returns colours palette. x Optional vector map colours . character/factor, returns named colour vector. numeric, interpolates along palette. reverse Logical, reverse colour order. Default FALSE. alpha Numeric 0-1, colour transparency. Default 1 (opaque).

title: Get Colours from a Named Palette — pal_get
text: character vector hex colours (named x provided).

code: plt_cat(   data,   stat.by,   group.by = NULL,   split.by = NULL,   type = c("bar", "rose", "ring", "pie", "trend", "area", "dot", "sankey", "chord",     "venn", "upset"),   stat = c("percent", "count"),   position = c("stack", "dodge"),   palette = NULL,   alpha = 0.8,   label = FALSE,   label.size = 3.5,   label.fg = "black",   label.bg = "white",   label.bg.r = 0.1,   bg.by = NULL,   bg_palette = NULL,   bg_alpha = 0.15,   flip = FALSE,   NA_color = "grey80",   NA_stat = FALSE,   keep_empty = FALSE,   stat_level = NULL,   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL,   xlab = NULL,   ylab = NULL,   legend.position = "right",   legend.direction = "vertical",   aspect.ratio = NULL,   facet_nrow = NULL,   facet_ncol = NULL,   facet_byrow = TRUE,   base_size = 14,   force = FALSE,   ... )

title: Unified Categorical Variable Plot — plt_cat
text: data data frame (Seurat object metadata). stat.Character. Column name(s) categorical variable summarise. standard types (bar, rose, ring, pie, trend, area, dot) single column name. set types (sankey, chord, venn, upset) character vector >= 2 column names. group.Character. Optional grouping variable standard types. Ignored set types. split.Character. Optional splitting variable. Splits data, creates one plot per level, combines patchwork. type Chart type. One "bar", "rose", "ring", "pie", "trend", "area", "dot", "sankey", "chord", "venn", "upset". stat Statistic compute: "percent" (default) "count". position Bar position: "stack" (default) "dodge". palette Colour palette. One : NULL (default): uses pal_lancet. single string matching name palette_list: uses     pal_get(). character vector colours: used directly. alpha Numeric 0–1. Colour transparency. Default 0.8. label Logical. Show value labels? Default FALSE. label.size Numeric. Label text size. Default 3.5. label.fg Character. Label foreground colour. Default "black". label.bg Character. Label background colour. Default "white". label.bg.r Numeric. Label background corner radius. Default 0.1. bg.Character. Column name background grouping dodge mode. Must superset group.(group.level belongs exactly one bg.level). Adds alternating background colour bands. Default NULL (background). bg_palette Character vector background band colours. Default NULL uses alternating c("grey85", "transparent"). bg_alpha Numeric 0–1. Background band transparency. Default 0.15. flip Logical. Flip coordinates bar type? Default FALSE. NA_color Character. Colour NA values. Default "grey80". NA_stat Logical. Include NA frequency table? Default FALSE (NAs dropped). TRUE, NAs kept shown NA_color. keep_empty Logical. Keep empty factor levels plots? Default FALSE. stat_level Character list. venn/upset types, value(s) stat.column count "positive". Default NULL auto-detects TRUE, "Yes", "yes", 1, "TRUE", "1", uses first factor level. title Character. Plot title. Default NULL. subtitle Character. Plot subtitle. Default NULL. xlab Character. X-axis label. Default NULL. ylab Character. Y-axis label. Default NULL (auto-set based stat). legend.position Legend position: "right" (default), "left", "bottom", "top", "none". legend.direction Legend direction: "vertical" (default) "horizontal". aspect.ratio Numeric. Panel aspect ratio. Default NULL (auto). Automatically set 1 rose, ring, pie types. facet_nrow Integer. Number rows combining split panels. Default NULL (auto). facet_ncol Integer. Number columns combining split panels. Default NULL (auto). facet_byrow Logical. Fill split panels row? Default TRUE. base_size Numeric. Base font size theme_my(). Default 14. force Logical. Force plotting variables > 100 levels? Default FALSE. ... Additional arguments passed type-specific plot function.

code: set.seed(1) df <- data.frame(   Type  = factor(sample(c("A","B","C"), 200, TRUE)),   Group = factor(sample(c("X","Y","Z"), 200, TRUE)),   Batch = factor(sample(c("B1","B2"), 200, TRUE)) ) df$Region <- factor(ifelse(df$Group %in% c("X","Y"), "R1", "R2"))  # ===== 1. Bar ===== plt_cat(df, "Type", "Group", type = "bar")                      # stacked %  plt_cat(df, "Type", "Group", type = "bar", stat = "count",         position = "dodge")                                      # dodged count  plt_cat(df, "Type", "Group", type = "bar", flip = TRUE,         label = TRUE)                                            # flipped + labels  plt_cat(df, "Type", "Group", type = "bar", position = "dodge",         bg.by = "Region")                                        # background bands  plt_cat(df, "Type", "Group", type = "bar", position = "dodge",         bg.by = "Region",         bg_palette = c("lightblue", "lightyellow"))              # custom bg colors  plt_cat(df, "Type", type = "bar", stat = "count",         label = TRUE, legend.position = "none")                  # no group   # ===== 2. Rose ===== plt_cat(df, "Type", "Group", type = "rose")  plt_cat(df, "Type", "Group", type = "rose", stat = "count",         position = "dodge")   # ===== 3. Ring (donut) ===== plt_cat(df, "Type", type = "ring") #> Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_col()`).  plt_cat(df, "Type", type = "ring", label = TRUE,         palette = "Paired") #> Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_col()`).  plt_cat(df, "Type", "Group", type = "ring", stat = "count",         position = "dodge") #> Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_col()`).   # ===== 4. Pie ===== plt_cat(df, "Type", type = "pie", label = TRUE)  plt_cat(df, "Type", type = "pie", palette = "Paired",         title = "Type Distribution", subtitle = "n = 200")  plt_cat(df, "Type", type = "pie", legend.position = "bottom",         legend.direction = "horizontal")   # ===== 5. Trend ===== plt_cat(df, "Type", "Group", type = "trend", stat = "count")  plt_cat(df, "Type", "Group", type = "trend", stat = "count",         label = TRUE)   # ===== 6. Area ===== plt_cat(df, "Type", "Group", type = "area", stat = "count")  plt_cat(df, "Type", "Group", type = "area", stat = "count",         position = "dodge")   # ===== 7. Dot ===== plt_cat(df, "Type", "Group", type = "dot")  plt_cat(df, "Type", "Group", type = "dot", label = TRUE,         stat = "count")   # ===== split.by / NA / keep_empty / aspect ===== plt_cat(df, "Type", "Group", split.by = "Batch", type = "bar")  plt_cat(df, "Type", split.by = "Batch", type = "pie",         facet_ncol = 2)   # NA handling df_na <- df; df_na$Type[1:20] <- NA plt_cat(df_na, "Type", "Group", type = "bar",         NA_stat = TRUE, NA_color = "red")   # keep_empty: preserve dropped levels df2 <- df[df$Type != "C", ] plt_cat(df2, "Type", "Group", type = "bar", keep_empty = TRUE)   # aspect.ratio plt_cat(df, "Type", "Group", type = "bar", aspect.ratio = 0.5)   # ===== 8. Sankey ===== plt_cat(df, c("Type", "Group"), type = "sankey") #> Warning: attributes are not identical across measure variables; they will be dropped  plt_cat(df, c("Type", "Group", "Batch"), type = "sankey") #> Warning: attributes are not identical across measure variables; they will be dropped   # ===== 9. Chord ===== plt_cat(df, c("Type", "Group"), type = "chord")    # ===== 10. Venn ===== set.seed(42) df_bin <- data.frame(   A = sample(c("Yes","No"), 100, TRUE),   B = sample(c("Yes","No"), 100, TRUE),   C = sample(c("Yes","No"), 100, TRUE) ) plt_cat(df_bin, c("A","B","C"), type = "venn")  plt_cat(df, c("Type","Group"), type = "venn", stat_level = c("A","X"))   # ===== 11. UpSet ===== plt_cat(df_bin, c("A","B","C"), type = "upset") #> Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0. #> ℹ Please use `linewidth` instead. #> ℹ The deprecated feature was likely used in the ggupset package. #>   Please report the issue at <https://github.com/const-ae/ggupset/issues>.  plt_cat(df, c("Type","Group"), type = "upset", stat_level = c("A","X"))

Default "wilcox.test". multiplegroup_comparisons Logical. Add global comparison? Default FALSE. multiple_method Character. Global test method. Default NULL. y.nbreaks Integer. Number y-axis breaks. Default 5. sort Logical character. Sort groups median? TRUE "decreasing" descending, "increasing" ascending. Default FALSE. stack Logical. The package "Hmisc" is required.  plt_con(df, "value1", "group", type = "dot")   # ===== Overlays ===== plt_con(df, "value1", "group", add_box = TRUE)  plt_con(df, "value1", "group", type = "box", add_point = TRUE)  plt_con(df, "value1", "group", type = "bar", add_trend = TRUE) #> Warning: Computation failed in `stat_summary()`. #> Caused by error in `fun.data()`: #> ! The package "Hmisc" is required.  plt_con(df, "value1", "group", add_point = TRUE, add_box = TRUE)   # ===== Multiple features ===== plt_con(df, c("value1", "value2"), "group")  plt_con(df, c("value1", "value2"), "group", stack = TRUE)  plt_con(df, c("value1", "value2"), "group", fill.by = "feature")   # ===== Statistical comparisons ===== plt_con(df, "value1", "group",         comparisons = list(c("A", "B"), c("A", "C")))  plt_con(df, "value1", "group",         multiplegroup_comparisons = TRUE, sig_label = "p.format")   # ===== Layout ===== plt_con(df, "value1", "group", flip = TRUE)  plt_con(df, "value1", "group", split.by = "batch")  plt_con(df, "value1", "group", bg.by = "region")  plt_con(df, "value1", "group", sort = TRUE)   # ===== Y-axis control ===== plt_con(df, c("value1", "value2"), "group",         same.y.lims = TRUE, y.max = 4)  # }

title: Parse Confidence Interval Strings — stat_ci_parse
text: x Character vector CI strings (e.g. "1.23 (0.95, 1.59)"). output return: "ci" (default) CI string (adjusted level set). "p" Numeric p-value vector. "ci_p" CI string p-value appended. "ci_star" CI string significance stars. "p_star" Formatted p-value stars. level Target confidence level CI adjustment (e.g. 0.90). Default NULL keeps original 95% CI. exp Exp-transformation detection: "auto" (default), TRUE, FALSE. digits Integer, decimal places output. Default: auto-detect input. map_signif Named numeric vector star thresholds. used output "ci_star" "p_star".

title: Parse Confidence Interval Strings — stat_ci_parse
text: Character vector (ci/ci_p/ci_star/p_star) numeric vector (p).

title: Parse Confidence Interval Strings — stat_ci_parse
code: # Basic: return CI as-is stat_ci_parse("1.23 (0.95, 1.59)") #> [1] "1.23 (0.95, 1.59)"  # Extract p-value stat_ci_parse("2.45 (1.20, 4.80)", output = "p") #> [1] 0.01128313  # CI with stars stat_ci_parse(c("2.45 (1.20, 4.80)", "1.23 (0.95, 1.59)"), output = "ci_star") #> [1] "2.45 (1.20, 4.80)*" "1.23 (0.95, 1.59)"   # CI with p-value stat_ci_parse("2.45 (1.20, 4.80)", output = "ci_p") #> [1] "2.45 (1.20, 4.80), p=0.011"  # Adjust confidence level stat_ci_parse("1.23 (0.95, 1.59)", level = 0.90) #> [1] "1.23 (0.99, 1.53)"  # In mutate() # df %>% mutate(p = stat_ci_parse(hr_ci, output = "p")) # df %>% mutate(hr_star = stat_ci_parse(hr_ci, output = "ci_star"))

title: Compute Cohen's d Effect Size by Group — stat_cohen
text: data data frame. group Character, name grouping variable (factor). vars Character vector variable names compare. onehot Logical. TRUE (default), categorical variables one-hot encoded computing Cohen's d. col_labels Optional named character vector renaming variables output (e.g. c(age = "Age (years)")).

title: Compute Cohen's d Effect Size by Group — stat_cohen

title: Compute Cohen's d Effect Size by Group — stat_cohen
code: # Basic: continuous variables stat_cohen(iris, group = "Species",            vars = c("Sepal.Length", "Sepal.Width")) #>       Variable     setosa versicolor  virginica #> 1 Sepal.Length -1.1324106  0.1214746  0.9487421 #> 2  Sepal.Width  0.8772742 -0.7024173 -0.2028886  # With column label mapping stat_cohen(iris, group = "Species",            vars = c("Sepal.Length", "Sepal.Width"),            col_labels = c(Sepal.Length = "Sepal L.", Sepal.Width = "Sepal W.")) #>   Variable     setosa versicolor  virginica #> 1 Sepal L. -1.1324106  0.1214746  0.9487421 #> 2 Sepal W.  0.8772742 -0.7024173 -0.2028886  # Mixed variables with one-hot encoding df <- data.frame(   group = factor(sample(c("A","B","C"), 100, TRUE)),   age = rnorm(100, 50, 10),   sex = factor(sample(c("M","F"), 100, TRUE)),   stage = factor(sample(c("I","II","III"), 100, TRUE)) ) stat_cohen(df, group = "group", vars = c("age", "sex", "stage")) #>    Variable            A           B           C #> 1       age  0.152936711 -0.04949980 -0.03634952 #> 2     sex_M -0.009060075 -0.03737443  0.03041184 #> 3   stage_I  0.017522616  0.07194424 -0.05930769 #> 4  stage_II  0.007510451  0.03094770 -0.02524949 #> 5 stage_III -0.024969200 -0.10397398  0.08286605  # Without one-hot (numeric variables only) stat_cohen(df, group = "group", vars = "age", onehot = FALSE) #>   Variable         A          B           C #> 1      age 0.1529367 -0.0494998 -0.03634952

title: UtilsR 0.2.0
text: stat_ci() — Build reformat CI strings mean(SD). Accepts numeric character input. stat_pval() — Format p-values stars, threshold display, add stars value. stat_ci_parse() — Parse CI strings, auto-detect exp transformation, compute p-values, adjust confidence levels. fct_cat() — Unified factor manipulation: recode, reorder, reverse, binary, group, combine. fct_num() — Numeric factor via cut points quantile/equal binning. fmt_plot() — Master ggplot formatting function chaining axis, tag, legend, ref. fmt_axis() — Hide/show axis elements multi-plot layouts. fmt_tag() — Add panel labels (, B, C…) plots. fmt_legend() — Format legend position, direction, merge across patchwork. fmt_ref() — Add reference lines per-line colors. fmt_strip() — Add coloured facet strip labels. fmt_com() — Add pairwise statistical comparisons via ggpubr. fmt_bg() — Add coloured background stripes. fmt_his() — Add marginal histogram/density overlay. fmt_scale() — Set axis scales auto-detection. fmt_expand() — Set axis expansion. fmt_boxplot() — Overlay boxplot layer violin/jitter plots. fmt_point() — Unified point/jitter/beeswarm layer auto-dodge white border. flatten_patchwork() — Recursively flatten nested patchwork objects. show_color() — Display colour swatches RStudio console. as_palette() / pal_list() — Create browse colour palettes. theme_my() / theme_km() / theme_rcs() / theme_legend1() — ggplot2 themes.
